Meng Guang (Xiaoyu)
孟光 (孝裕)

Meng Guang, styledXiaoyu, was fromLuoyang[洛陽],Henan[河南]. He was a kinsman of theGrand CommandantunderHan,Meng Yu.A
TheBook of Continued Hanstates:[Meng] Yuwas the younger brother ofRegular Palace AttendantMeng Ben [孟賁].
During the end [of the reign] of theKeenEmperor, he served as Subofficial who Lectures, and when theAstuteEmperormoved to settle the capital atChang’an[長安], he fled and entered [蜀].Liu Yanand hissontreated him with the rites owed to a guest. He was rich in information of various subjects and possessed knowledge of the past, with there being no book that he did not read. He was especially focused on the Three Histories1and was experienced with the past institutions of theHan dynasty. He liked theCommentary of Gongyangon theChroniclesand ridiculed theCommentary of Zuo. Whenever he contended withLai Minover the meanings of those two,[Meng] Guangwould often argue loudly, clamoring andshouting.B•••
They were theScribal Records,Book of Han, andEastern Tower Annals of Han.↩
Nao [譊; argue] has the fanqie of Nu [奴] and Jiao [交]. Huan [讙; clamoring] has the fanqie of Xiu [休] and Yuan [袁]. Zha [咋; shouting] has the fanqie of Cu [徂] and Ge [格].
After theFormer LordstabilizedYi province[益州], he was conferred to serve asGentleman Consultant. He and the likes ofXu Ciwould all direct regulations and measures.
After theLatter Lordascended the throne steps, he served asDirector of the Tallies,Colonel of the Garrison Cavalry, andMinister Steward of Changle Palace, then promoted toMinister of Finance.
In the ninth year of theera [246], autumn, there was a grand amnesty.[Meng] Guang, amidst a crowd, rebuked theGrand General,Fei Yi. He said, “Amnesties intrinsically favor one side and harm the other. They should not exist in eras of brilliance. When entropy reaches its most extreme extent, leaving no other choice, only then may they be deliberated and undertaken. At present, theSovereignis benevolent and worthy, and the officials are qualified in their duties. How can there be imminent peril and grave urgency?2Yet, you have conducted numerous acts of unnatural clemency. Is this to benefit the wickedness of treacherous rebels? Moreover, hawks and falcons are beginning to strike.3To nevertheless forgive those with crimes is an affront against natural temporality above and an violation against human principles below. I, an old man, am a decaying octogenarian with no comprehension of the tenets of governance, but even I believe that this program is difficult to sustain for a long period. How can this be the noble beauty of widespread excitement or the brilliant virtue that is hoped for!”[Fei] Yiwould only turn to apologize to him in a respectful but uneasy manner and nothingmore.•••
Literally “peril of dawn and dusk, and the urgency of being hung upside down”.↩
During the end of summer and towards autumn, hawks and falcons began to hunt down their prey. This is probably an allusion to the pardon happening in the autumn. In addition, “hawks and falcons” is a metaphor used to describe violent people.↩
Whenever[Meng] Guangsignalized and criticized what was harmful or irritating, they were often like this case. Therefore, the important ministers who controlled governance were unable to be content in their hearts and did not raise him in peerage nor rank. Whenever he spoke candidly, he shied away from nothing. He was thus despised by his generation. The likes of theMinister of Ceremonies,Tan ChengofGuanghan[廣漢],Cand theMinister of the Household,Pei JunofHedong[河東], were behind[Meng] Guangin years and service, yet they rose to hold paramount positions. That they were ranked more superior than[Meng] Guangwas probably due tothis.D
TheRecords of Huayang Statestates:[Tan] Chengwas styledGongwen. He served asCommandery Administratorin different areas andMinister Steward.
TheFamily Records of the Pei Clanby Fu Chang [傅暢] states:[Pei] Junwas styledFengxian. He was the younger brother of theDirector of the SecretariatunderWei,[Pei] Qian. The husband of the older sister of[Pei] Junserved asChief Clerkin Shu [蜀], and[Pei] Junescorted him, being over ten years old at the time. It happened that there was great disorder towards the end of theHan, and so he could not return. When he matured, he had a renowned reputation, and was promoted and valued by Shu [蜀]. His son,[Pei] Yue, was styledLingxu. He served asArmy MarshalforShu. WhenShufell, he returned toLuoyang[洛陽] and was conferred to serve asGentleman Consultant.
Later, a newcomer literary scholar,Gentleman SecretaryXi Zheng, would consult[Meng] Guangon numerous occasions.[Meng] Guangasked[Xi] Zhengabout what theHeir Apparentstudied and recited, as well as his nature and predispositions.[Xi] Zhengreplied, “He treats his parents with humility and respect. From morning to night, he is never negligent, having the bearing of the heir childes of antiquity. In how he receives and treats the officials, his actions and movements show benevolence and empathy.”[Meng] Guangsaid, “That which you have described is possessed by every household. I had asked my question just now because I want to learn about what sort of pragmatic plans and wise calculations he has.”[Xi] Zhengsaid, “The way of the heir childe is in adhering to [parental] wishes and delighting to the utmost, not making arbitrary initiatives. Wise calculations are contained within the heart, while pragmatic plans are done according to the moment. How could their existence or absence then be presumed in advance?”[Meng] Guangdeduced that[Xi] Zhengwas being cautious and orthodox, not talking freely, so he said, “I like to speak candidly, shying away from nothing. Every time I make direct condemnations to correct maladies, I am ridiculed and despised by my peers. I understand your thoughts, sir. You do not like what I have to say, but my spiel has structure to them. At present, the realm is not yet stable, and so wisdom is important. Even though wisdom is innate, it can be obtained by vigorous efforts. Thus, whenheir apparentrecites books, might he also devote his efforts to having a rich memory in emulation of us, so he can await inquiries and questions, and to research questions for him to be examined and tested as the academians do, so he can seek peerage and rank? This is a most urgent situation!”[Xi] Zhengthought that what[Meng] Guangsaid was right.
Later,[Meng] Guangwas implicated in matters and dismissed from office. When he was over ninety years old, he died.
